events.description: Release pipeline started: js - netapp - mgmt

insights.description: 1.28-day gap between spec PR merge (Feb 11) and first SDK PR creation (Feb 12) — AutoPR triggered quickly
3.87-day wait for first human review on spec PR — audunn opened Jan 26, first review from razvanbadea-msft on Jan 30
.NET SDK PR was manually authored (by nandakus2), took 14.07 days — manual flow has more review cycles compared to automated PRs
Mixed generation flow: .NET was manual (human-authored), Java/Go/Python/JS were automated (AutoPR). All 5 SDK PRs merged and released within ~3 weeks of spec merge.
1 author nag(s) detected — spec owner had to ping reviewers for action on SDK PRs
All 5 SDK packages released with avg 3.0-day gap from merge to release (max 4.6 days for .NET)

39 MCP tool calls tracked for .NET SDK development — build/test/check cycles from both human (VS Code) and AI agent (github-copilot-developer). Multiple build failures before first success on Mar 2, followed by agent-assisted builds on Mar 10.
.NET release pipeline failed on Mar 22 (build 6045300) before succeeding on Mar 25 (build 6061571). Total release gap: 19.3 days.
